channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Clayton Kershaw to return to Los Angeles Dodgers: reports
item.description: Los Angeles Dodgers legend Clayton Kershaw is returning to the defending World Series champions for the 2025 season.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Steve Smith Sr. unhappy with former teammate Cam Newton calling Panthers 'losers' prior his arrival
item.description: CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — Former Carolina star wide receiver Steve Smith Sr. took exception to comments made by ex-teammate Cam Newton, who called the Panthers “losers” before he arrived in 2011 as the franchise's No. 1 overall pick.
item.dc:creator: STEVE REED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Luka Doncic makes his Lakers debut and quickly connects with LeBron James in a 132-113 win over Utah
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Luka Doncic was the last starter introduced by the Los Angeles Lakers, an honor normally reserved for LeBron James.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NASCAR's new 'world-class driver' rule makes Daytona 500 qualifying more complicated than ever
item.description: DAYTONA BEACH, Fla. (AP) — The Daytona 500 has NASCAR’s most unique — and complicated — qualifying format. And it got more convoluted this year with the largest number of entrants in a decade and a new rule that could add a 41st driver to the 40-car field.
item.dc:creator: MARK LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fan fest in Jersey City will be open for all 104 World Cup matches next year
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A fan fest at Liberty State Park in Jersey City, New Jersey, will be open for all 104 games of the 2026 World Cup. Fan fests with large video screens have been a part of each World Cup's organization since 2006.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thoughts on the Chicago Bears from around the NFL on Radio Row during Super Bowl week
item.description: Chicago Bears new head coach Ben Johnson is expected to bring a fresh perspective to the team, while quarterback Caleb Williams is expected to elevate his game with the help of Johnson's expertise.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Sabathia to have Yankees logo on Hall of Fame plaque, Wagner the Astros, Suzuki the Mariners
item.description: COOPERSTOWN, N.Y. (AP) — CC Sabathia will have a New York Yankees logo on the cap of his Hall of Fame plaque and Billy Wagner will have the symbol of the Houston Astros. The hall announced the decisions Monday for all five of this year's inductees.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tiger Woods withdraws from Genesis Invitational after death of mom
item.description: Tiger Woods announced on Monday he will no longer participate in the Genesis Invitational as he's still "processing" the death of his mother.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Super Bowl returning to the Bay Area for 2026
item.description: (KRON) -- The Super Bowl is coming back to the Bay Area next year. A decade ago, the region hosted Super Bowl 50. Next year, it will play host to Super Bowl LX. On Monday morning, San Jose Mayor Matt Mahan was in New Orleans for the official Super Bowl handoff.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Eagles deny the Chiefs a Super Bowl three-peat with dominant defense in a 40-22 rout
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Yo Philly: Jalen Hurts and Nick Sirianni are world champions. A ferocious Philadelphia Eagles defense tormented and frustrated Patrick Mahomes while Hurts made all the plays the offense needed. So much for the Kansas City Chiefs’ quest for a Super Bowl three-peat.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Eagles beat Chiefs 40-22 in lopsided Super Bowl LIX
item.description: The Kansas City Chiefs wanted to make history with three back-to-back-to-back championships, but Super Bowl LIX was dominated by the Philadelphia Eagles, who won the game 40-22.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Utes offense struggles again in loss to West Virginia
item.description: MORGANTOWN, W.V. (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah basketball team shot just 41 percent from the field and lost its third straight road game at West Virginia Saturday, 72-61.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Utah HC gives up season-high 7 goals to Carolina
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (ABC4 Sports) - After two overtime thrilling victories, the Utah Hockey Club gave up a season-high seven goals in a 7-3 loss Saturday to the Carolina Hurricanes. Seth Jarvis had two goals and two assists, and Sebastian Aho had two goals and an assist for the Hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Padres sign outfielder/first baseman Connor Joe for 2025 season
item.description: The San Diego Padres have made a move to bolster their roster for the upcoming season by signing outfielder and first baseman Connor Joe to a one-year contract, President of Baseball Operations and General Manager A.J. Preller announced Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Amber Coakley, Fox 5

item.title: Martinez scores 30 as USU beats Fresno State, 89-81
item.description: FRESNO, Calif. (ABC4 Sports) - Ian Martinez scored 30 points and led Utah State to an 89-81 victory over Fresno State Friday night. Martinez, who made 10 of 14 shots from the field, tied a career high with eight rebounds for the Aggies (21-3, 11-2 Mountain West Conference).
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Gatorade colors, on-field proposals and other bets before Super Bowl
item.description: Known as prop bets, the annual list of side wagers includes game-related stats as well as creative propositions.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: Jazz acquire guard KJ Martin at trade deadline
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - It was a busy trade deadline for the Utah Jazz, but when all the dust settled, the Jazz are coming away with one new player and five draft picks.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: NCAA changes participation policy on transgender athletes
item.description: The NCAA has changed its participation policy for athletes, keeping transgender students from women's sports
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: NCAA changes transgender policy to limit women's competition to athletes assigned female at birth
item.description: The NCAA changed its participation policy for transgender athletes on Thursday, limiting competition in women's sports to athletes assigned female at birth only.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Jason Kelce and look-alike posse take on New Orleans
item.description: Five of the most passionate NFL fan duos in America were challenged to be the first team to find the real Jason Kelce.
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Virginia Halas McCaskey, longtime Bears owner and matriarch, dies at 102, team announces
item.description: LAKE FOREST, Ill. — Virginia Halas McCaskey, the longtime owner and matriarch of the Chicago Bears, died Thursday at 102, the team announced in a news release. Virginia served as the owner of the Bears since her father and team founder George S. Halas died on Oct. 31, 1983.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: McDavid makes perfect pass in overtime as the Oilers beat the Blackhawks 4-3
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Connor McDavid set up Zach Hyman in overtime, and the Edmonton Oilers beat the Chicago Blackhawks 4-3 on Wednesday night. Hyman redirected McDavid's pass for the power-play goal at 1:36. It was Hyman's 19th goal and McDavid's 49th assist of the season.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Jimmy Butler headed to Golden State, Andrew Wiggins to Miami, AP source says
item.description: Jimmy Butler got what he wanted. He's being traded out of Miami and got a new contract in the process. The Heat and the Golden State Warriors have agreed on a deal that sends Butler to the Bay Area, a person with knowledge of the talks said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Warriors to trade for Miami's Jimmy Butler: ESPN
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- The Golden State Warriors are finalizing a blockbuster trade for Miami Heat star Jimmy Butler, ESPN reported Wednesday. Butler, 35, also agreed to an extension with the Warriors that will keep him with the team through the 2026-27 season, ESPN said.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: Local football player overcomes hardships, signs to play Division I football
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — Mental toughness isn't something you're born with; it's a learned skill. One that Calvary Christian senior Nelson Sparrow Jr. has already mastered. "It feels great," he said. "I dreamt of this. I worked hard for this every single day of my life.
item.dc:creator: Kenny Morales, WFLA, NBC
